data.title: XRP Crashes 40%, Before Recovering, in Biggest One-Day Drop
data.description:
XRP experienced a significant decline of 40% in its value during a single day, marking the largest drop recorded within a 24-hour period. However, following this dramatic plunge, the cryptocurrency made a notable recovery, regaining some of its lost value as traders and investors reacted to the market's volatility.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2025/10/11/xrp-crashes-40-before-recovering-in-biggest-one-day-drop

data.title: A heavily invested Chinese meme address suffered a floating loss of $3.39 million, accounting for nearly 80% of Binance’s total losses.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi's monitoring, a certain address's six Meme token positions on BSC have accumulated a floating loss of US$3.39 million, of which the unrealized loss on Binance Life is as high as US$2.71 million, accounting for 79.7% of the total floating loss. Only Hakimi's position is in a floating profit state.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Surge Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that if Bitcoin's price rises above $118,000, the cumulative liquidation intensity of short positions on major centralized exchanges (CEX) could reach $2.078 billion.

It is important to note that the liquidation chart does not precisely display the number of contracts pending liquidation or the exact value of liquidated contracts. Instead, the chart's bars represent the relative importance of each liquidation cluster compared to nearby clusters, indicating intensity.

Therefore, the chart illustrates the potential impact on the market when the asset price reaches a certain level. A higher 'liquidation bar' suggests that reaching this price point will trigger a stronger reaction due to a wave of liquidity.

data.title: Hyperliquid Hit by Major Exploit After Private Key Compromise
data.description:
Blockchain investigators from PeckShield revealed that attackers accessed a private key linked to Hyperliquid’s Hyperdrive lending protocol.
source: https://coindoo.com/hyperliquid-hit-by-major-exploit-after-private-key-compromise/

data.title: Lighter: The platform service interruption is due to technical problems caused by extremely high TPS, and user funds are safe.
data.description: Lighter officials posted on X that due to extremely high TPS over the past 12 hours, the platform experienced a service outage within the past hour. The team is working tirelessly to resolve the technical issue. A resolution is nearing completion, and user funds are safe, with no security or risk issues.

data.title: CZ: The "hunting of market maker Wintermute" is not true. We only provide trading tools.
data.description: Regarding the community rumor that "CZ participated in hunting Wintermute", Binance founder CZ responded on X: "I haven't read or heard (or thought of) the word "Wintermute" for several months. The last time I read this name was on X. It is not something I have in mind. Trading (as a profession) is not what I/we do. We provide trading tools for others. Don't believe false narratives."

data.title: The amount of liquidation on the Hyperliquid platform in the past 24 hours has exceeded US$10 billion, setting a new record high
data.description: Coinglass data shows that the amount of liquidated positions on the Hyperliquid platform has exceeded $10 billion in the past 24 hours, currently reaching $10.297 billion, of which $9.317 billion were from long positions and $980 million were from short positions. Furthermore, the total amount of liquidated positions across all exchanges in the past 24 hours reached $19.301 billion, with $16.808 billion from long positions and $2.493 billion from short positions.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs Experience Mixed Inflows and Outflows
data.description:
According to Foresight News, data from SoSoValue indicates that on October 10, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $4.5038 million.

The Bitcoin spot ETF with the highest single-day net inflow was Blackrock's IBIT, which recorded an inflow of $74.2134 million. The historical total net inflow for IBIT has reached $65.26 billion.

Conversely, the Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Bitwise's BITB, which saw an outflow of $37.4488 million. BITB's historical total net inflow stands at $2.483 billion.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $158.965 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 6.98% compared to Bitcoin's total market capitalization. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $62.77 billion.

data.title: Data: Bitmine's ETH holdings have a floating loss of approximately $1.93 billion, with an average purchase price of $4,535
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, as the price of ETH fell below $3,900, Bitmine, a publicly listed company, suffered a significant blow to its ETH holdings, currently facing a floating loss of approximately $1.93 billion. Currently, Bitmine holds 2,830,151 ETH, purchased at an average price of $4,535 with a total capital of $12.83 billion. Its current ETH holdings are valued at $10.9 billion.

data.title: The number of on-chain addresses on the Gate Layer has exceeded 45 million, a 95.7% surge in 24 hours.
data.description: Official data indicates that the Gate Layer ecosystem continues to expand. Driven by the launch of Gate Fun and Gate Perp DEX applications, the number of on-chain addresses has exceeded 45 million, a 95.7% surge in 24 hours, demonstrating a steady increase in on-chain activity. Gate Layer is reportedly a high-performance second-layer network independently developed by Gate. Leveraging LayerZero for multi-chain interoperability, Gate Layer supports cross-chain asset transfers with major public chains such as Ethereum, BSC, and Polygon. With a design philosophy of "low barrier to entry, high efficiency, and low cost," Gate Layer is committed to providing convenient on-chain services for users and developers.

data.title: JustLend DAO community releases JST repurchase and destruction proposal
data.description: JustLend DAO has published a JST buyback and burn proposal on the governance forum. This proposal aims to establish a more sustainable deflationary mechanism by optimizing the JST token's economic structure, enhancing governance efficiency, and long-term value. The proposal states that buybacks will primarily be funded by JustLend DAO's net revenue (including sTRX and SBM net revenue) and incremental revenue from the USDD multi-chain ecosystem once profits exceed $10 million. All buybacks will be transparently executed on-chain, and JST tokens received will be permanently destroyed to gradually reduce the circulating supply. Furthermore, the DAO plans to continue buybacks on a quarterly basis using incremental net revenue and provide regular public progress reports. Officials stated that this mechanism will further strengthen JST's governance and promote healthier and more resilient growth for the TRON ecosystem.
